summary: Stellar late‑'79 energy with historic Scarlet>Fire stack and tight jams
blurb: The show bursts open with a punchy Alabama Getaway and stays hot through a fiery first set that includes Friend Of The Devil and Dancin' In The Street. A uniquely stacked second set delivers Scarlet Begonias > Fire On The Mountain, Passenger, Terrapin Station and Playing In The Band back‑to‑back. Brent Mydland’s fresh keyboards and Phil Lesh’s booming ‘Phil Zone’ drive the performance.
review: On November 30, 1979 at Pittsburgh’s Stanley Theatre the Dead were in full flight, just weeks into Brent Mydland’s tenure. Jerry’s guitar cut cleanly through a crystal‑clear board mix, from the bright opening jam on Alabama Getaway to his searing lead on the Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain transition and an inspired solo during Playing In The Band. Phil Lesh owned the low end – reviewers rave about his “Phil Zone” attacking Alabama Getaway and propelling the groove throughout both sets. Brent’s keyboards added a fresh, front‑and‑center texture that lifted songs like Dancin' In The Street and Friend Of The Devil, while Mickey and Billy locked in a powerful dual‑drum foundation that kept the second‑set momentum soaring even through the lengthy Terrapin Station jam. Highlights include the historic stacked sequence of Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Passenger → Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band, a smoking Friend Of The Devil, and a high‑energy Dancin' In The Street that showcases the band’s late‑’70s firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six soundboard recordings and the matrix source are marked positive, with repeated descriptors such as “stellar,” “smoking,” “fantastic,” and “high‑energy.” Multiple reviewers highlight the same standout songs (Scarlet>Fire stack, Dancin' In The Street, Friend Of The Devil) and consistently praise the tight band interplay, indicating strong, uniform enthusiasm. This warrants a robust positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent separation, minimal tape noise and a mix that showcases the full energy of the historic stacked second set.

key_highlights: Historic stacked second‑set sequence: Scarlet Begonias → Fire On The Mountain → Passenger → Terrapin Station → Playing In The Band.
Smoking Friend Of The Devil and high‑energy Dancin' In The Street jam in the first set.
Brent Mydland’s fresh keyboard textures lifting the band early in his tenure.
Phil Lesh’s powerful ‘Phil Zone’ bass driving Alabama Getaway and the entire show.

band_performance.Jerry: Clean, bright guitar that sits perfectly in the mix; superb leads on Scarlet>Fire and a standout solo during Playing In The Band.
band_performance.Phil: Front‑and‑center bass dubbed the ‘Phil Zone,’ attacking Alabama Getaway and driving the groove throughout both sets.
band_performance.Bob: Generally solid rhythm work; brief vocal lapse noted on El Paso but no major issues.
band_performance.Brent: Bright, front‑and‑center keyboard textures that add fresh energy to the set, especially on Dancin' In The Street and Friend Of The Devil.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful drumming that propels the early energy and provides a solid foundation for the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Tight dual‑drum work with Mickey, delivering a strong backbone especially during the Terrapin Station and Space sections.
